rulings.Case Title: LOUIS BOLANOS VS. ONE WEST BANK et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 25, 2013, line 1. - DEFENDANTS JR GROUP, INC., DBA KELLER WILLIAMS AND TRI-VALLEY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant JR Group's Demurer to Complaint is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND within 10 days of Notice of Entry of Order. Prevailing Party to Prepare Order. Non-opposition filed. = (D501/HD)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY D. MURPHY VS. MICHAEL YAKOVLEVICH KLESTOFF et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 25, 2013, line 2. DEFENDANTS MICHAEL KLESTOFF AND WEST & PRASZKER REALTY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is 1) SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to 4th, 5th, 6th, 7th and 8th causes of action added to First Amended Complaint without leave of Court and in excess of the scope of permitted amendment per Order sustaining Demurrer to 8/20/12 Complaint with leave to Amend. Contrary to Plaintiff's assertion, in ruling on Defendants' demurrer the Court considered only challenges to causes of action alleged in the complaint and not the whole universe of potential unalleged counts that Plaintiff might have against Defendants; the Court granted Plaintiff a leave to amend to address those challenges/issues. Harris v. Wachovia (2010) 185 Cal.App.4th 1018. 2) SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as to 1st cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotion Distress to allege outrageous conduct exceeding all bounds of decency, not mere insults, indignities, threats, annoyances, petty oppression or other trivialities. CACI 1602. 3) OVERRULED as to 2nd and 9th causes of action. Whether or not repairs were "necessary" is an issue of fact not properly raised on demurrer. 4) SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as to 3rd cause of action to allege facts showing violation(s) of CCC 1940.2(a)(1) [facts showing violation of Penal Code 484(a)]. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from the Notice of Entry of Order to file an amended complaint. Prevailing Party to Prepare Order. = (D501/HD)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY D. MURPHY VS. MICHAEL YAKOVLEVICH KLESTOFF et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 25, 2013, line 2. DEFENDANTS MICHAEL KLESTOFF AND WEST & PRASZKER REALTY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's Motion to Strike First Amended Complaint is moot. = (D501/HD)

rulings.Case Title: MICKEY Y WONG VS. DORIS WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Compliance With Document Requests; To Compel Further Responses To Document Requests; For Protective Order; & For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 25, 2013, line 3. - DEFENDANT DORIS WONG Motion for Compliance With Document Requests; To Compel Further Responses To Document Requests; For Protective Order; & For Sanctions. Defendant's Motion for Compliance with Document Requests, to Compel Further Responses; for Protective Order and Sanctions. Hearing Required. Parties are ordered to appear at 9:00 a.m. to engage in further meet and confer efforts. Parties to report to the court at 9:30 a.m. = (D501/HD)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY VERMILLION VS. MARGARITA MORENO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Ptlf'S Further Responses To Written Discovery And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 25, 2013, line 4. - DEFENDANT 1930 MISSION STREET CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Further Responses To Written Discovery And Request For Sanctions. Defendant 1930 Mission Street Condominium Association's Motion to Compel Further Responses is GRANTED. Plaintiff shall serve further verified responses without objections no later than July 29, 2013 by 4 p.m. by personal service. Sanctions are grated against plaintiff and Mr. Hammond in the amount of $2,971 to be paid to Defendant's counsel no later than July 31, 2013 by 4 p.m. = (D501/HD)

rulings.Case Title: RKI INVESTMENTS, LLC VS. EDWARD JELINEK et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 25, 2013, line 6. DEFENDANTS EDWARD JELINEK AND SAMUEL ACKERMAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's' Demurrer to Complaint is moot as to Defendant Edward Jelinek. Dismissal on file 7/22/2013. Defendant Ackerman's Demurrer is OVERRULED. Demurrer raises issued of fact not appearing on the face of complaint. Defendants' counsel is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. = (D501/HD)

rulings.Case Title: MOHAMMED IRSHAD et al VS. MILDRED VEGA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 25, 2013, line 8. DEFENDANTs MILDRED VEGA AND ROGER INTERIAN Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. Defendants' Motion to Quash Service of Summons is GRANTED. Plaintiff failed to sustain burden. Non-registered process server is not entitled to presumption. = (D501/HD)
